microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE they like food warm but not overly hot. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE fatigue makes comfort and warmth more valuable. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE speed outweighs ideal temperature. IF social setting with a person to the left or right AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE they want normal meal quality without extra delay. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet in front AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE attention is divided but warmth still matters. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND fruit or cold dessert THEN no microwave BECAUSE those foods are intended to be served cold. IF social setting AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE this user prioritizes comfort under fatigue over social efficiency.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food without special viewing demand THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE there is no strong competing visual target. IF social setting with a person to the left AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE they want to keep that partner in view. IF social setting with a person to the right AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE they want to keep that partner in view. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the screen is the primary visual target. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE task visibility matters. IF any social or screen-focused context AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN keep the same left/right/front minimization BECAUSE this user treats environmental visibility as more important than saving motion. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE efficiency outweighs view management when no one or nothing important is being watched.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE they want balanced efficiency and comfort. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND any food THEN fast BECAUSE they tolerate automation and want to reduce meal time. IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE fatigue increases desire for comfort and predictability. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND food requiring careful bite acquisition due to limited mouth opening THEN slow BECAUSE smaller, more precise transfers feel safer.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet personal setting AND neutral AND elongated or larger solid foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE narrower bites suit limited mouth opening. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND elongated or larger solid foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE comfort and easier eating outweigh acquisition convenience. IF social setting with a person to the left or right AND in a hurry AND hard-to-acquire slippery foods THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this user accepts larger bites when speed and acquisition success both matter. IF watching television or working on a laptop/tablet in front AND neutral AND hard-to-acquire foods THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE divided attention favors reliable pickup. IF any dining context AND neutral AND bite-sized or easy-to-skewer foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE there is no need to trade comfort for acquisition success. IF social setting AND physically fatigued AND hard-to-acquire foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE this user consistently prioritizes low-effort eating under fatigue over social neatness or speed.
